rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains arithmetic claims whose internal consistency must be verified (e.g., 0.84 − 0.62 = 0.22, not the stated 0.35 absolute improvement; cost comparisons: 41% vs 55% requires checking whether 'strictly lower cost' holds for each comparison direction). Logical soundness of the threshold-selection argument and ablation inference also fall here.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing under 'Check this' for a quantitative systems paper: every numerical claim (quality scores, cost fractions, pool-size generalization) must be traceable to the described experimental setup. The '35% absolute quality improvement' figure directly contradicts the 0.84 vs 0.62 figures in the same paragraph, making this dimension the highest-stakes axis.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly asserts 'substantial advance over prior routing approaches' and frames LaneRouter as establishing a new state of the art. Verifying whether only two baselines (uniform random and frontier-only) constitute a fair comparison against the claimed prior art is central to any meaningful check.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I prioritize verifying the internal quantitative claims and evidence-to-number alignment (R1.3 load-bearing), or assessing whether the novelty and contribution framing is justified relative to the broader prior-work landscape (R1.4 load-bearing)?
